title: 0/1 knapsack maximum value given weights, values, and a capacity

informal_statement.text:
Given equal-length slices of non-negative item weights and item values, and a non-negative integer capacity, return the maximum total value achievable by choosing a subset whose total weight does not exceed the capacity. Each item may be selected at most once.

Benchmark note (dp_knapsack_01_007): 0/1 knapsack optimum (variant 7): emphasize exponential brute-force agreement on tiny instances as a behavioral anchor.
Authoring lens: Lens 7 aligns informal witness semantics with exhaustive subset enumeration.
preconditions: weights and values have the same length
every weight is a non-negative integer
every value is a non-negative integer
required_properties: the result is witnessed by some subset S of item indices whose total weight <= capacity and total value equals the result
no subset whose total weight <= capacity has a strictly greater total value
each item index is used at most once in any witness subset
edge_cases: capacity 0 (result is 0 unless there is a zero-weight positive-value item)
a single item with weight > capacity
multiple items of identical weight and value
